name: Fort Walton Beach Golf Course
address: 1955 Lewis Turner Blvd, Fort Walton Beach, FL 32547
about: Fort Walton Beach Golf Club is a public facility on Florida's Emerald Coast featuring two 18-hole championship courses and a modern TrackMan-equipped driving range. The Pines Course offers a classic parkland layout with rolling fairways, while the Oaks Course provides a tighter, tree-lined design with water hazards and a scenic Southern feel. The venue also includes a full-service clubhouse, pro shop, and restaurant, catering to both locals and visitors with exceptional value and year-round play.
why_golfers_like_it: Advanced TrackMan technology for real-time shot data and simulated rounds
Two distinct 18-hole championship courses offering varied challenges
Exceptional value compared to other local resort courses
Year-round play availability with warm, sunny Florida weather

food_and_drink.notes: Restaurant likely serves alcohol; pro shop may have beverages

simulator_hardware.notes: Cutting-edge TrackMan Range technology for real-time shot data, virtual games, and simulated rounds

faq.question: Do I need my own clubs?
faq.answer: While not explicitly stated, as a golf course, players typically bring their own clubs. Rental clubs may be available through the pro shop.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drink?
faq.answer: The venue has a full-service restaurant and bar on-site. Outside food and drink policies are not specified, but typically allowed on the courses.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: Standard 18-hole play time is typically 4-5 hours depending on pace of play and course conditions.

faq.question: Is the facility kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, the venue welcomes families with multiple tee boxes for different skill levels and a full-service clubhouse atmosphere suitable for all ages.
